title: Sleep is not something I do. It happens to me
subtitle: A release-and-allow statement for releasing the effort of trying to sleep.
excerpt: A short energy statement for when you are in bed trying to make yourself sleep. The release targets the effort; the allow targets the state of receiving sleep rather than chasing it.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 4 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The release-and-allow structure draws on The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice the effort of trying to sleep: the watching, the waiting, the willing. Say it as a signal to stop the effort.

content.text: A short energy statement for the moment you catch yourself trying to make sleep happen. Say it slowly, in bed, as an instruction to stop the effort.
I am ready to release the effort of trying to make sleep happen. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align with and allow sleep to arrive without my effort. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say each three times, slow, in your mind or just above a whisper.
Adapted from Day 4 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive to Fall Asleep Faster and Wake Energised (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The release-and-allow structure draws on The Money Zone (Rebecca Page, 2024).
